project_context.core_aesthetic: Aggressive white space, decluttered layouts, typography-driven, focus on primary values, high-contrast, navy blue accents, color-coded financial data.

upload_illustration.description: Minimalist 3D abstract illustration for the PDF upload screen, representing clean document data extraction.

instructions_to_main_agent: CRITICAL: This is a React Native Expo project. DO NOT USE HTML elements (div, span, p) or Tailwind CSS classes. Use React Native components (View, Text, TouchableOpacity, ScrollView, FlatList, Image) and StyleSheet.create().
Use expo-router for navigation between screens (Upload -> Format Detection -> Preview -> Summary/Download).
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a 'testID' attribute (e.g., testID='upload-button'). Do not use 'data-testid' as it is not valid in React Native.
Implement the UI using a mobile-first, thumb-friendly approach. Primary actions should be at the bottom of the screen.

For the Transaction Preview Table, use a FlatList. It must be paginated (50 rows/page). Color-code amounts: Red text with light red background for debits, Green text with light green background for credits, Yellow/Amber for mismatches.
For the Summary Statistics Dashboard, use a Bento-grid style layout with flexbox (`flexWrap: 'wrap'`, `flexBasis: '48%'` for side-by-side metrics).
Since memory-only processing is required, mock the PDF extraction and AI detection logic heavily, but structure the state to hold the 'extracted' JSON data in memory via React Context or Zustand.
To download Excel in React Native, use a library like `xlsx` and `expo-file-system` + `expo-sharing` to allow the user to save/share the generated file.
Use @expo/vector-icons (e.g., Ionicons or Feather) for all icons.
Follow the generous spacing guidelines. Avoid cluttered screens. Emphasize the data.
